releases.name: Attu v2.6.5
releases.body:
## Attu v2.6.5

 New Features
  - AI-Powered Schema Generation: A new "AI" tab in the Create Collection dialog lets users describe their use case in natural language and get an AI-recommended collection schema. Supports multiple
  AI providers (including DeepSeek) with in-app API key management and model selection.
  - File Upload for Schema Prediction: Upload a CSV or JSON file (up to 10MB) in the AI chat panel when creating a collection. The AI analyzes the file structure to automatically detect field types —
   including arrays and vector fields — and suggests an appropriate schema.
  - Modify Replica Count for Loaded Collections: In distributed deployments, users can now adjust the replica count of a loaded collection directly from the collection header without having to
  release and reload it.
  - Default Timezone Set to UTC: Database and collection creation now defaults to UTC timezone instead of the user's local timezone, ensuring consistent behavior across teams and environments.

  Bug Fixes
  - Fixed Authentication for Milvus System Views: System monitoring pages (Tasks, Segments, Replicas, Slow Queries, etc.) now correctly pass auth credentials when connected to a Milvus instance with
  authorization enabled, resolving 401 errors.
  - Fixed Task Grid Row Rendering: The Tasks view (Build Index, Compaction, Import, etc.) previously had duplicate React keys causing jumbled or incorrect row display. Each task grid now uses the
  correct task-specific identifier.
  - Fixed _default Partition Name Display: The default partition now correctly displays as _default, matching the actual name in Milvus APIs (previously shown as "Default").
  - Playground Security Hardening: Patched SSRF and DNS rebinding vulnerabilities in the Playground feature to prevent unauthorized requests to internal network resources.

releases.name: Attu v2.6.4
releases.body:
## Attu v2.6.4

This release introduces several new features including AI embedding generation and NGRAM index support, alongside significant performance improvements for handling large numbers of databases and collections. It also includes various bug fixes for timezone handling, UI responsiveness, and data import/export.

## 🚀 New Features
- **AI Embedding Support**: Added AI embedding generation for vector search fields and removed restrictions on embedding model selection.
- **NGRAM Index**: Support for creating NGRAM indexes on `VarChar` and `JSON` fields.
- **Database Management**: Enhanced the Home page with database sorting functionality and improved UI management.
- **Nginx Support**: Added configuration files and scripts for specific Nginx deployment scenarios.

## ⚡ Performance Improvements
- **Scalability**: Optimized `listDatabase` to gracefully handle a large number of databases.
- **UI Responsiveness**: Improved rendering performance for the Databases page and Collection creation form by reducing unnecessary re-renders and stabilizing state updates.
- **Data Refresh**: Unified batch collection refresh logic to prevent redundant API calls.

## 🐛 Bug Fixes
- **Timezone Handling**: Multiple fixes to ensure correct timezone persistence, default values (UTC), and correct display logic across database properties.
- **Authentication**: Fixed an issue where auth form input state was lost when switching tabs.
- **Data Import/Export**:
  - Supported both schema and simplified formats for JSON imports.
  - Removed `db_name` from downloaded schema JSON for better portability.
  - Corrected binary vector generation and validation.
- **UI/UX**:
  - Resolved issues with database switching and collection count display.
  - Fixed URL formatting when clicking the Attu logo.
  - Disabled window shadow on macOS Tahoe to prevent GPU load issues.
  - Improved partition selection and deletion interactions.
- **Query & Search**: Fixed quoting for `VarChar` IDs in node search query expressions.
- **Schema**: Prevented NGRAM index creation on Struct fields where it isn't supported.

## 🛠 Miscellaneous
- **Dependencies**: Bumped `mdast-util-to-hast` to 13.2.1.

### Downloads
- **macOS**: Download the `.dmg` file for macOS
- **Linux**: Download the `.deb` file for Linux
- **Windows**: Download the `.exe` installer for Windows

releases.name: Attu v2.6.3
releases.body: Attu v2.6.3 introduces **new Timestamptz data type support**, an advanced filter expression editor with AST-based parser, database cloning functionality, and improved UI/UX. This release also includes custom webui API address configuration in the login form, timezone support, and redesigned homepage and connect page. ## 🎉 New Features ### New Data Type: Timestamptz - **Timestamptz Data Type Support**: Full support for Timestamptz data type including schema creation, filter expressions with ISO format validation, arithmetic/comparison operators, timezone conversion, and local time display with tooltips ### Connection & Authentication - **Custom WebUI Address**: Users can now customize the webui API base address in the login/connect form - **Language Selector**: Added language selector to the login page - **Enhanced Connect Form**: Improved connection form UI with advanced options ### Filter Expression Editor - **Advanced Filter Editor**: New CodeMirror-based editor with real-time validation, AST-based parser, and enhanced syntax highlighting - **New Operators**: Added TEXT_MATCH function, ST_COVERS/ST_ISVALID geometry operators, arithmetic operators, and INTERVAL literal support - **i18n Support**: Internationalization for filter operators and parser messages ### Database Management - **Clone Database**: Added ability to clone databases - **Improved Database UI**: Enhanced database card and collection info cards layout ### User Interface - **Redesigned Homepage**: Complete redesign with Zilliz Cloud promotion button and improved community links - **Timezone Support**: Comprehensive timezone support with localized date/time formatting - **Enhanced Search Page**: Improved search page state management and user experience ## 🔧 Improvements - Replaced CollectionData with CollectionDataV2 and useQueryV2 hook for better performance - Optimized component layouts and extracted reusable components - Enhanced dark mode support and improved tooltip positioning - Custom CodeMirror theme for search input ## 🐛 Bug Fixes - Fixed webui_api_base handling and managed service detection - Fixed data clearing when switching connections or logging out - Fixed DOM nesting warnings and dark mode visibility issues - Fixed Firefox autocomplete positioning ## 📦 Dependencies - Updated `@zilliz/milvus2-sdk-node` from `2.6.4` to `2.6.5` ## 🔄 Migration Notes No breaking changes. This is a backward-compatible release. Users can upgrade seamlessly from v2.6.2 to v2.6.3.

releases.name: Attu v2.6.2
releases.body:
## Attu v2.6.2

This release includes new features, improvements, and bug fixes since version 2.6.1. The update brings struct data type support, enhanced UI components, and various improvements to the user experience.

## 🎉 New Features

### Major Refactoring: Collection Creation & Schema Management
- **Struct Data Type Support** (#89): Complete refactoring of collection creation and schema pages to support struct data types, including:
  - Redesigned collection creation interface with tree-based field editor
  - Full support for nested struct fields and struct arrays
  - Enhanced schema page with improved field management
  - Auto-load collections immediately after creation
  - Dynamic field toggle functionality with refactored feature chips

### Data Type & Index Support
- **AISAQ Index Type**: Added configuration support for AISAQ index type

### Collection & Data Management
- **Filter Expression Highlighting**: Added syntax highlighting for filter expressions in grid view
- **Field Icons & Tooltips**: Enhanced DataListView with field icons and informative tooltips, moved copy button to content area
- **Default Varchar Search String**: Updated default varchar search string for better usability

## 🔧 Improvements & Refactoring

### UI/UX Enhancements
- Optimized dark mode colors for better visibility
- Improved CustomDrawer header spacing and size
- Extracted CollectionHeader and CollectionInfoCards components from Schema for better code organization

## 📦 Dependencies & Infrastructure

### Dependency Updates
- Upgraded Milvus SDK to v2.6.2
- Upgraded react-syntax-highlighter to v16.1.0
- Bumped validator from 13.9.0 to 13.15.23 in server (#90)

## 🔄 Breaking Changes
None

readme_html:
Attu

Attu is the premier administration tool for Milvus. From visual schema design to enterprise-grade monitoring, manage your vector database with ease.

Comprehensive Data & Schema Management

  • Visual Schema Designer: Design, view, and modify collection schemas with an intuitive visual interface. Access full property details at a glance.
  • Full Lifecycle Control: Effortlessly manage multiple databases, collections, and their properties at scale. Instant cloning, seamless configuration, and streamlined operations.
  • Smart Data Operations: Powerful data management with smart filters, syntax highlighting, complete data viewing, inline editing, and seamless import/export capabilities.

Interactive Development Toolkit

  • Interactive Vector Similarity Search with Visualization
  • Fast Expression-based Data Querying
  • Integrated RESTful API Editor
  • Seamless Support for Milvus

Enterprise Security & Monitoring

  • Visualized RBAC & Privilege Group Management
  • Real-time Node, Segment & Task Monitoring
  • Slow Query Analysis & System Diagnostics
  • Detailed Segment Query & Inspection

Table of Contents

  • Features
  • System Requirements
  • Quick Start
  • Installation Guides
    • Compatibility
    • Running Attu from Docker
    • Running Attu within Kubernetes
    • Running Attu behind a nginx proxy
    • Install Desktop application
  • Development
  • Contributing
  • FAQ
  • More Screenshots
  • Useful Examples
  • Milvus Links
  • Community

System Requirements

  • Docker 20.10.0 or later
  • Kubernetes 1.19 or later (if using K8s deployment)
  • Modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
  • For desktop application:
    • Windows 10/11
    • macOS 10.15 or later
    • Linux (Ubuntu 20.04 or later)

Quick Start

  1. Start Milvus server (if not already running):
docker run -d --name milvus_standalone -p 19530:19530 -p 9091:9091 milvusdb/milvus:latest
  1. Start Attu:
docker run -p 8000:3000 -e MILVUS_URL=localhost:19530 zilliz/attu:v2.6
  1. Open your browser and navigate to http://localhost:8000

Installation Guides

Before you begin, make sure that you have Milvus installed on either Zilliz Cloud or your own server.

Compatibility

Milvus Version Recommended Attu Version
2.6.x v2.6.3
2.5.x v2.5.10
2.4.x v2.4.12
2.3.x v2.3.5
2.2.x v2.2.8
2.1.x v2.2.2

Running Attu from Docker

Here are the steps to start a container for running Attu:

docker run -p 8000:3000 -e MILVUS_URL={milvus server IP}:19530 zilliz/attu:v2.6

Make sure that the Attu container can access the Milvus IP address. After starting the container, open your web browser and enter http://{ Attu IP }:8000 to view the Attu GUI.

Optional Environment Variables for Running Attu Docker

Parameter Example Required Description
MILVUS_URL 192.168.0.1:19530 false Optional, Milvus server URL
DATABASE your database false Optional, default database name
ATTU_LOG_LEVEL info false Optional, sets the log level for Attu
ROOT_CERT_PATH /path/to/root/cert false Optional, path to the root certificate
PRIVATE_KEY_PATH /path/to/private/key false Optional, path to the private key
CERT_CHAIN_PATH /path/to/cert/chain false Optional, path to the certificate chain
SERVER_NAME your_server_name false Optional, name of your server
SERVER_PORT Server listen port false Optional, 3000 by default if unset

Please note that the MILVUS_URL should be an address that the Attu Docker container can access. Therefore, "127.0.0.1" or "localhost" will not work.

To run the Docker container with these environment variables, use the following command:

Attu SSL Example

docker run -p 8000:3000 \
-v /your-tls-file-path:/app/tls \
-e ATTU_LOG_LEVEL=info  \
-e ROOT_CERT_PATH=/app/tls/ca.pem \
-e PRIVATE_KEY_PATH=/app/tls/client.key \
-e CERT_CHAIN_PATH=/app/tls/client.pem \
-e SERVER_NAME=your_server_name \
zilliz/attu:v2.6

Custom Server Port Example

This command lets you run the docker container with host networking, specifying a custom port for the server to listen on

docker run --network host \
-v /your-tls-file-path:/app/tls \
-e ATTU_LOG_LEVEL=info  \
-e SERVER_NAME=your_server_name \
-e SERVER_PORT=8080 \
zilliz/attu:v2.6

Running Attu within Kubernetes

Before you begin, make sure that you have Milvus installed and running within your K8's Cluster. Note that Attu only supports Milvus 2.x.

Here are the steps to start a container for running Attu:

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zilliztech/attu/main/attu-k8s-deploy.yaml

Make sure that the Attu pod can access the Milvus service. In the example provided this connects directly to my-release-milvus:19530. Change this based on the Milvus service name. A more flexible way to achieve this would be to introduce a ConfigMap. See this example for details.

Running Attu behind a nginx proxy

Running Attu behind a nginx proxy

Install Desktop application

If you prefer to use a desktop application, you can download the desktop version of Attu.

Note:

  • Mac M chip install app failed: attu.app is damaged and cannot be opened.
  sudo xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine /Applications/attu.app

FAQ

  • I can't log into the system

    Make sure that the IP address of the Milvus server can be accessed from the Attu container. #161

  • If you encounter issues installing the desktop app on Mac OS, refer to the note under Install Desktop application.
  • How to update Attu?

    For Docker users, simply pull the latest image and restart the container. For desktop users, download the latest release from our releases page.

  • How to backup my Attu configuration?

    Attu configurations are stored in your browser's local storage. You can export them from the settings page.

Milvus links

Here are some helpful resources to get you started with Milvus:

  • Milvus documentation: Here, you can find detailed information on how to use Milvus, including installation instructions, tutorials, and API documentation.
  • Milvus python SDK: The Python SDK allows you to interact with Milvus using Python. It provides a simple and intuitive interface for creating and querying vectors.
  • Milvus Java SDK: The Java SDK is similar to the Python SDK but designed for Java developers. It also provides a simple and intuitive interface for creating and querying vectors.
  • Milvus Go SDK: The Go SDK provides a Go API for Milvus. If you're a Go developer, this is the SDK for you.
  • Milvus Node SDK: The Node SDK provides a Node.js API for Milvus. If you're a Node.js developer, this is the SDK for you.
  • Feder: Feder is a JavaScript tool designed to aid in the comprehension of embedding vectors.

Community

💬 Join our vibrant community on the Milvus Discord where you can share your knowledge, ask questions and engage in meaningful conversations. It's not just about coding, it's about connecting with other like-minded individuals. Click the link below to join now!
